quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Properties of a Wave
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question: The crest of a transverse wave is the:
options: Point of maximum displacement below the equilibrium position
Point of maximum displacement above the equilibrium position
Number of waves passing a point per second
questions.explanation: The crest is the highest point of a transverse wave — the point of maximum positive displacement from equilibrium.

questions.question: The trough of a transverse wave is the:
options: Point of maximum displacement above the equilibrium position
Point of maximum displacement below the equilibrium position
Region where particles are closest together
questions.explanation: The trough is the lowest point — the point of maximum negative displacement from equilibrium.

questions.question: In a longitudinal wave, a compression is a region where:
questions.explanation: A compression is a high-pressure region where the particles of the medium are bunched closest together.

questions.question: In a longitudinal wave, a rarefaction is a region where:
questions.explanation: A rarefaction is a low-pressure region where particles are spread apart, following a compression.

questions.question: The period of a wave is defined as the:
options: Number of waves passing a point per second
Maximum displacement of wave from the equilibrium position
Time taken for one complete wave to pass a fixed point
Distance between two consecutive crests or troughs
questions.explanation: Period ($T$) is the time for one complete oscillation or for one full wavelength to pass a point.

questions.question: The frequency of a wave is defined as the:
options: The total time taken for one complete oscillation of the wave
Maximum displacement of the wave from the equilibrium position
Number of complete cycles passing a fixed point per second
Distance between two consecutive crests or troughs
questions.explanation: Frequency ($f$) is the number of complete wave cycles passing a point per second, measured in hertz (Hz).

questions.question: If the period of a wave is $0.025\text{ s}$, what is its frequency?
A wave has a frequency of $500\text{ Hz}$. What is its period?

A displacement-time graph of a wave is shown below.

What is the amplitude of the wave?
questions.explanation: The amplitude is the maximum displacement from the equilibrium line to a crest or trough. From the graph, this distance is $2.0\text{ m}$.

questions.question:
A displacement-time graph of a wave is shown below.

What is the period of this wave?
questions.explanation: The period is the time for one complete cycle. From the graph, one full oscillation takes $0.4\text{ s}$.

questions.question: The equilibrium position of a wave is the position where:
questions.explanation: The equilibrium position is the rest position — the midline of the wave where displacement is zero.

questions.question: Two points on a wave are in phase if they: (Select all that apply)
options: Are separated by half a wavelength
Have the same displacement at the same time
questions.explanation: Points in phase vibrate identically — they have the same displacement and move in the same direction at the same instant. They are separated by whole multiples of a wavelength. Having the same amplitude alone or both being at zero displacement is not sufficient.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the properties and terminology of waves!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review compression vs. rarefaction and the period-frequency relationship.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on the definitions of crest, trough, amplitude, wavelength, period, and frequency.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: parts of a wave, $f = 1/T$, and the difference between displacement-time and displacement-position graphs.
